injuryUpdates.injuryType: Recovered from December 2025 knee surgery and April 15 right-knee blow vs Porto
injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Fit and selected — named captain of the 26-man tournament squad

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Started for Nottingham Forest; Porto's Jan Bednarek sent off in the 5th minute for a tackle that caught Wood's right knee. Wood has since recovered and been declared fit.

injuryUpdates.source: ESPN, NZ Herald, Friends of Football — May 13-14, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Bazeley confirmed Wood fit by including him in the 26-man squad announced May 13. Wood was named captain — becomes one of two All Whites (with Tommy Smith) to play at two tournaments. Returned from December knee surgery, then took a knock vs Porto on April 15 but has recovered. Cleared status from 'doubt' to 'fit'.
Included in the 26-man tournament squad. No injury concerns reported.
Included in the 26-man tournament squad. No injury concerns reported.

squadChanges.details: Veteran defender (age 36, Braintree Town in English non-league) recalled to the All Whites for the first time since late 2024. Will become one of two New Zealand men (with Chris Wood) to play at two tournaments.
Surprise selection: 23-year-old Newcastle Jets midfielder named in the 26-man squad just two months after making his international debut. Australian-born, qualifies through his father.
Wellington Phoenix defender omitted from the final 26-man squad despite being a regular through the qualifying cycle.
Orlando Pirates striker left out of the final 26-man squad.
Huddersfield Town goalkeeper omitted from the final 26-man squad; Michael Woud (Auckland FC) edged him and Max Crocombe for a keeper spot, with Crocombe and Alex Paulsen also included.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: BetMGM and bet365, aggregated via Squawka/TheLines — May 12, 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Among the longest outright prices in the field (~1000/1 to 1500/1 to win). Group-stage advancement price is competitive given the 32-team knockout format that lets four best third-placed sides through; advancement essentially hinges on the Iran fixture.

summary: New Zealand named their final 26-man squad on May 13-14 with Chris Wood confirmed fit and installed as captain after recovering from December knee surgery and an April 15 right-knee knock vs Porto — a clear positive shift from his pre-tournament 'doubt' status. Veteran defender Tommy Smith (Braintree Town, 36) was recalled for the first time since late 2024 and joins Wood as a two-time tournament player, while 23-year-old Newcastle Jets midfielder Lachlan Bayliss is a notable surprise call-up; Bill Tuiloma, Andre de Jong and Nik Tzanev were the highest-profile omissions. No competitive matches have been played since April 16; the All Whites face Haiti (June 3, Fort Lauderdale) and England (June 7, Tampa) as warm-ups before opening Group G play vs Iran on June 16.
